what_it_does: This design document is the application-space scout (B3) for the concurrent-cognition series. It assumes the six-part mechanism designed in threads 00-06 and asks where else the same primitive can be applied inside the Company and beyond. It maps fifteen candidate application fields (A-O), evaluates each by reuse vs net-new and a concurrency quality gate, identifies the highest-leverage reuses, surfaces four 'reshape-the-core' design tensions (R1-R4), and lists open questions for Tim.
observations: Contains a 15-row application map table with fields, role definitions, unlock claims, reuse/net-new split, and a concurrency gate.

Defines the reusable mechanism as six composable parts plus seven invariants, including resident model, swarm of roles, structured JSON outputs as addresses, composition edges, MODE budget, self-instrumentation, and live rendering.
Epistemic tags follow doc 06's model: Observed, Designed, Open; status is open/provisional/discovery-scout.

Highest-leverage reuses are called out as K (document/codebase map-reduce), M (typed-triage classification), I (altitude transformation layer), F (introspective-data rollups), and B (background cognition).

Four reshape-the-core findings: R1 ensemble/jury needs a per-draw-variation primitive; R2 role-vs-claude -p subagent autonomy boundary; R3 output_destination governance is more load-bearing than four sinks; R4 budget should be keyed by activation context, not only presence mode.
Includes a relational diagram showing applications feeding each other and F (rollups) closing the feedback loop.
Voice reply (A) is treated as the seed/concrete instance, not the only use.
Beyond-the-Company (O) is flagged as the furthest, product-direction question.
Hard constraint cited: worker roles must never fire claude -p or arm acceptEdits; only supervised lead acts.
No B1/B2 files exist in broader/ at time of writing; doc is self-contained and cross-links the thread files.
standouts: The 'reshape-the-core' section (R1-R4) surfaces missing primitives that several high-value applications need, rather than just listing use cases.
The leverage framework (unlock magnitude × reuse fraction) is used to prioritize applications.
The explicit autonomy boundary (roles propose, lead acts) bounds the seductive recursion of cognition coordinating its own builds.
The altitude transformation layer (I) is flagged as a high-value off-list application whose absence signals the list is a floor.
concerns: Almost every application is Designed/Open, so the doc is highly speculative and not a build plan.
Leverage and concurrency-gate ratings are qualitative and not quantified.
Many applications depend on unbuilt pieces (vision model residency, ensemble primitive, activation-context budgets).
The reuse-vs-net-new column mixes architectural reuse with role-set additions without clear scoring thresholds.
Section O ('beyond the Company') introduces product scope that may be out of bounds for the current design series.
notes: A forward-looking scout that turns the voice mechanism into a general cognition substrate and uses it to expose four design tensions that must be resolved before broad reuse.
questions: Which application is intended as the second proving target after voice: K, M, or F?
Will the ensemble primitive (R1) be added to the role registry now or deferred?
How will the output_destination field accommodate 'propose/surface for review' and typed-lane/channel routing?
Is budget keying by activation context (R4) accepted, or will MODE remain tied to presence modes only?
Will the role-vs-subagent boundary (R2) be enforced structurally or remain a convention?
What criteria distinguish 'strong' from 'medium' concurrency gates?
How will a vision-capable resident model share the 16GB card with the brain?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to scout where else the concurrent-cognition mechanism unlocks value; the document does exactly that, plus prioritizes by leverage and surfaces design tensions.
apparent_themes: Reuse over net-new: add role rows, not systems.
Concurrency + composition as a general cognition substrate.
Self-instrumentation and feedback loops (use → record → knowledge → better use).
Field-agnostic primitive pointed at new domains.
Bounded autonomy: roles think/propose, supervised lead acts.
One source of truth via the registry.
intention_signals: The file is trying to persuade Tim that the concurrent-cognition engine is a reusable Company-wide substrate, identify the cheapest high-value places to point it next, and force explicit decisions about missing core primitives before they become hidden assumptions.
novelty: A systematic application-space inventory combined with a leverage=reuse×unlock heuristic and an explicit 'reshape-the-core' section that derives missing design primitives from cross-application composition rather than from a single use case.
contribution: A structured catalog of candidate application fields, a leverage-based prioritization heuristic, and four concrete design tensions (R1-R4) that any fused build plan for the concurrent-cognition substrate must resolve.
summary_for_embedding: B3 is a design scout that maps fifteen broader applications of the concurrent-cognition role-swarm mechanism across the Company, prioritizes them by reuse×unlock, and surfaces four core-design reshapes and open questions for Tim.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to show where the same concurrent-role primitive could be reused beyond voice and to surface the design decisions needed before those reuses can be built.
